canonical_physical_graph_policy.future_model_graph_role: The processed weighted physical graph W is retained for the proposed graph forecaster and graph-aware ablation studies.
canonical_physical_graph_policy.spatial_failure_cluster_role: Not used directly for cluster growth because its 34.56% undirected support density is too broad for strictly local outage construction.

intervention_scope.elapsed_since_observed_recomputation: Required after combining native and artificial missingness.
intervention_scope.model_invariant_scenario_policy: Each split-scenario corruption realization will be generated once and reused unchanged across all future model comparisons.

native_observation_policy.artificial_dropout_eligibility: Only positions that are true in the native observation mask and belong to the historical input context are eligible for artificial removal.
native_observation_policy.observation_semantics: A true entry is naturally observed and usable under the native availability convention.

randomness_policy.deterministic_cluster_growth: Spatial cluster expansion uses breadth-first search. At each expansion, neighbours are visited in ascending geographic distance, then ascending graph-position index to resolve ties deterministically.
randomness_policy.seed_derivation: SHA-256 over protocol ID, root seed, split name, and scenario ID; first eight digest bytes reduced modulo 2^32-1, with zero replaced by one.
randomness_policy.test_target_independence: Scenario randomness must be derived without reading future target values or target masks.

iid_random.mask_interpretation: Sampling is conditional on native availability. Existing native-missing positions are preserved and are never counted as artificially removed.
iid_random.removed_cell_count_rule: n_remove = floor(rate * n_eligible + 0.5), where n_eligible is the count of native-observed historical input cells in the window.
iid_random.sampling_rule: For each window, uniformly sample without replacement from native-observed historical positions.

spatial_cluster_outage.cluster_graph: Symmetric geographic k-nearest-neighbour graph with k=4.
spatial_cluster_outage.cluster_growth_rule: Use deterministic breadth-first growth until the requested connected cluster size is reached.
spatial_cluster_outage.cluster_seed_rule: Sample one graph-position seed uniformly from all 207 sensors.
spatial_cluster_outage.outage_interval_rule: Remove only native-observed entries for every cluster sensor across all 12 historical input steps.

temporal_tail_outage.outage_interval_rule: For each selected sensor, remove only native-observed entries in the final L historical steps, where L is the scenario outage length. The outage terminates immediately before the forecast origin.
temporal_tail_outage.target_sensor_rule: Eligible sensors have at least one native-observed cell in the selected historical tail interval.

scenarios.description: Native observation availability only. No artificial dropout.
Uniform random removal of exactly 10% of native-observed historical cells per window.
Uniform random removal of exactly 30% of native-observed historical cells per window.
Uniform random removal of exactly 50% of native-observed historical cells per window.

canonical_v2_execution_rule.intervention_rule: remove all native-observed tail positions for each selected sensor, namely all tail_length_steps cells
canonical_v2_execution_rule.zero_full_eligibility_policy: selected_sensor_count=0; preserve native historical context unchanged

scenarios.description: A 15-minute outage ending at the forecast origin for 25% of eligible sensors.

superseded_v1_temporal_eligibility_seman.reason_for_supersession: At-least-one-observed-tail eligibility may select a sensor with native missing entries inside the intended tail and therefore cannot guarantee an exact L-step artificial outage.

canonical_v2_execution_rule.intervention_rule: remove all native-observed tail positions for each selected sensor, namely all tail_length_steps cells
canonical_v2_execution_rule.zero_full_eligibility_policy: selected_sensor_count=0; preserve native historical context unchanged

scenarios.description: A 30-minute outage ending at the forecast origin for 50% of eligible sensors.

superseded_v1_temporal_eligibility_seman.reason_for_supersession: At-least-one-observed-tail eligibility may select a sensor with native missing entries inside the intended tail and therefore cannot guarantee an exact L-step artificial outage.

canonical_v2_execution_rule.intervention_rule: remove all native-observed tail positions for each selected sensor, namely all tail_length_steps cells
canonical_v2_execution_rule.zero_full_eligibility_policy: selected_sensor_count=0; preserve native historical context unchanged

scenarios.description: A 60-minute outage ending at the forecast origin for 75% of eligible sensors.

superseded_v1_temporal_eligibility_seman.reason_for_supersession: At-least-one-observed-tail eligibility may select a sensor with native missing entries inside the intended tail and therefore cannot guarantee an exact L-step artificial outage.

scenarios.description: A connected geographic-kNN4 sensor cluster of 5 sensors is unavailable throughout the full 60-minute historical input context.
A connected geographic-kNN4 sensor cluster of 10 sensors is unavailable throughout the full 60-minute historical input context.
A connected geographic-kNN4 sensor cluster of 20 sensors is unavailable throughout the full 60-minute historical input context.

construction.symmetrisation: An undirected local edge exists when either sensor selects the other among its k nearest geographic neighbours.
construction.tie_break_rule: Stable ascending geographic-distance order, then ascending graph-position index.

spatial_failure_topology.purpose: Failure-cluster generation only. It must not replace the canonical physical graph W used by the future graph-based forecasting model.

supersedes.protocol_v1_generation_status: Protocol v1 must not be used for controlled-dropout mask generation after protocol v2 is frozen.

temporal_tail_full_outage_execution_v2.artificial_dropout_rule: For every selected sensor, mark all L intended tail positions as artificial dropout. This guarantees a complete contiguous artificial outage of exactly L five-minute steps for every selected sensor.
temporal_tail_full_outage_execution_v2.elapsed_feature_policy: Recompute the causal elapsed-gap feature separately inside each corrupted 12-step historical window using the frozen native carry-in state and the exact elapsed addendum recurrence.
temporal_tail_full_outage_execution_v2.eligibility_predicate: A sensor is eligible for a tail length L only when all native observation-mask entries across the final L historical input timestamps are true.
temporal_tail_full_outage_execution_v2.secondary_cohort_rule: Generate each corruption realization once for the primary cohort. The secondary cohort must reuse the exact artificial-dropout mask from its matching ordered primary window. It must never independently sample sensors or use a separate random seed.
temporal_tail_full_outage_execution_v2.selection_rule: Sample selected_count sensors uniformly without replacement from the full-tail eligible sensor set, using the unchanged protocol-v1 scenario/window randomness convention.
temporal_tail_full_outage_execution_v2.zero_eligibility_rule: When no sensor is full-tail eligible in a primary window, selected_count is zero. Retain the native historical context unchanged and report zero effective artificial removal for that scenario-window.

test_cohort_pairing_policy.secondary_policy: The secondary cohort must reuse the exact corruption realizations generated for the primary windows. It must be obtained only by selecting the existing ordered primary-window subset indicated by secondary_flags_for_primary. Independent secondary dropout regeneration is prohibited.

v2_execution_precedence.canonical_rule_location: temporal_tail_full_outage_execution_v2 and each temporal scenario canonical_v2_execution_rule
v2_execution_precedence.conflict_resolution: For temporal_tail_outage scenarios, the v2 canonical execution rule overrides inherited protocol-v1 wording wherever a conflict exists. All non-temporal scenario definitions are inherited unchanged from protocol v1.
